title: Analysis Suggests School Was Hit Amid U.S. Strikes on Iranian Naval Base
summary: The Feb. 28 school strike in Minab, which killed dozens, including children, appears to have been part of an attack on an adjacent naval base in southern Iran, where officials said U.S. forces were operating.

title: Lebanon at ‘Tipping Point’ as It Seeks to Disarm Hezbollah
summary: The country is waiting to see if the government seizes on this moment to disarm the Iranian-backed armed group and how the militants will respond.

title: As Trump Out-Putins Putin, Russia’s Global Influence Erodes
summary: The conflict in Iran may give Moscow a short-term boost economically and in Ukraine. But it has also shown the limits of Russia’s partnerships.

title: Kristi Noem’s Firing, and the Boom of American Billionaires
summary: Plus, your Friday news quiz.

title: Timeline: The Rise and Fall of Kristi Noem as Trump’s DHS Secretary
summary: The homeland security secretary, who was fired by President Trump Thursday, helped fulfill his border pledges, but also drew negative attention to his administration.

title: Senator Blumenthal Pushes to Investigate Kristi Noem for Perjury
summary: Senator Richard Blumenthal of Connecticut said Democrats had evidence to suggest Kristi Noem lied under oath during a Senate hearing on Tuesday.

title: Trump Announces He Is Replacing Noem With Oklahoma Senator
summary: The president said he would replace Kristi Noem, the homeland security secretary, with Senator Markwayne Mullin, after Ms. Noem was grilled by Republicans at a congressional hearing.

title: Minnesota Governor Walz and Minneapolis Mayor Welcome Kristi Noem’s Firing
summary: Mayor Jacob Frey of Minneapolis said “Good riddance” after Ms. Noem’s ouster. Gov. Tim Walz and others said they still wanted a “complete overhaul” of the department.

title: Pardon Industry Offers Rich Offenders a Path to Trump
summary: One inmate paid lobbyists and lawyers with ties to the president’s team and walked free. Others are following his blueprint, but it is not always clear who can deliver.

title: Big Revisions Are a Reason to Question the Jobs Numbers, Not to Dismiss Them
summary: Economists say estimates from the Bureau of Labor Statistics and other agencies are reliable, but they worry the quality of data is eroding.

title: Health Care Has Become the Lifeblood of the Labor Market
summary: An aging population is drawing workers to medical and social care, creating reliable jobs and revealing weakness for the rest of the economy.

title: U.S. Gas Prices, Up 11% in a Week, Pile Pressure on Trump
summary: The average price of a gallon of gasoline jumped again on Friday, to $3.32 per gallon, hitting its highest level in a year and a half.

title: Alysa Liu on Dancing Her Way to Olympic Gold: ‘The Music Carries My Body’
summary: The free-spirited figure skater spoke to our dance critic about her approach to performing, the role of music and more.

title: Representative Tony Gonzales Will Not Seek Re-election
summary: Several House members had called for Mr. Gonzales, a Texas Republican, to step down after texts emerged showing his pursuit of a staff member who later killed herself.

title: A Candidate for Georgia Straight From the Marjorie Taylor Greene Playbook
summary: Colton Moore, who hopes to win a special election on Tuesday to replace Ms. Greene, has the same flair for the dramatic and the ultra-MAGA persona, but he lacks one thing: a Trump endorsement.

title: Trump Pick Of Mullin For D.H.S. Sets Off Political Scramble in Oklahoma
summary: The president’s choice of Senator Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma to replace her at the Department of Homeland Security could trigger a cascade of aspirants for his seat.

title: Steve Daines’s Switcheroo Starts New Fight in Montana Senate Race
summary: Minutes before the Senate filing deadline, Senator Steve Daines withdrew his re-election bid and an ally jumped in. Even some fellow Republicans criticized the 11th-hour switch.

title: Jesse Jackson Chicago Funeral: Obama, Biden, Clinton to Attend
summary: The large-scale, public event in Chicago on Friday caps two weeks of memorials for Mr. Jackson, the civil rights leader, who died at 84.

title: Jesse Jackson, Charismatic Champion of Civil Rights, Dies at 84
summary: An impassioned orator, he was a moral and political force, forming a “rainbow coalition” of poor and working-class people and seeking the presidency. His mission, he said, was “to transform the mind of America.”

title: Renters Made Mamdani Mayor. Can He Remake the City for Them?
summary: His call to ‘freeze the rent’ galvanized the 69 percent of New Yorkers who don’t own their homes. But the city’s landlords claim the math doesn’t add up.

title: The Allure of ‘Slop Bowls’ Fades as Consumers Tighten Spending at Restaurants
summary: Sales and traffic at restaurant chains like Cava, Chipotle and Sweetgreen are falling, as customers grow tired of both salad bowls and their rising price tags.

title: He Had a Purple Heart, PTSD and a Rap Sheet. He Had to Leave the U.S.
summary: After a long battle with drug addiction, Sae Joon Park felt settled in a new life. But he was deported last year and is now fighting to get back to Hawaii.

title: Jamil Jivani, a Friend of JD Vance, Channels Charlie Kirk With Young Men
summary: Jamil Jivani, a Conservative rookie member of Parliament, follows an American playbook to win over young men on college campuses.

title: After a Brutal Winter, Worse News for Oyster Farmers
summary: The deep freeze on the East Coast will still be felt during the summer. Long Island oysters will be in short supply.

title: The Good Times Are Over in China’s Liquor Capital
summary: Once celebrated for its fiery spirits, the town of Maotai has reeled from a bad Chinese economy, changing tastes and a crackdown on boozy official banquets.

title: Why the Pentagon Wants to Destroy Anthropic
summary: The former A.I. policy adviser to the Trump White House explains why the conflict between Anthropic and the White House is so dangerous.

title: Daryl Hannah: How Can ‘Love Story’ Get Away With This?
summary: The character “Daryl Hannah” in “Love Story” is not even a remotely accurate representation of my life or my conduct — and these kinds of lies don’t go away.

title: Late Night Isn’t Sad to See Kristi Noem Leave Homeland Security
summary: “Sounds like someone’s about to become the FIFA secretary of homeland security,” Stephen Colbert joked.

title: DOJ Releases Missing Interviews With Woman Who Made Claims Against Trump in Epstein Files
summary: The pages had been withheld from the trove of documents related to the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein because of what officials called a mistaken determination that they were duplicates.

title: Indonesia to Block Children Under 16 From Social Media
summary: The ban is to take effect March 28, according to a government minister, but details about how it would be implemented were scarce.

title: Potomac River in D.C. Is Safe, Officials Say. But Locals Still Worry About the Poop.
summary: A broken pipe sent a gusher of sewage into the river near Washington. Some people may try to row, sail or fish. But skeptics are steering clear.

title: 180,000 New Yorkers May Lose Food Stamp Benefits Under New Work Rules
summary: Social workers are scrambling to alert recipients and help them find jobs before their aid is eliminated under President Trump’s sweeping domestic policy law.
